title: Global Trade Growth
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
Over the past few years, the World Trade Organization's Global Trade Outlook and Statistics has become an essential barometer of the shifting tides in global commerce. In the wake of the severe disruptions of the early 2020s, world trade has gradually regained its footing, supported by the reconfiguration of supply chains and the return of industrial activity and consumer demand. Yet, this recovery has been far from uniform.
